team_news: Sweden have attacking upside through Viktor Gyokeres, Alexander Isak and Anthony Elanga, but Dejan Kulusevski is reported out and defensive stability is a concern.
Tunisia are described as highly organized defensively, with Ellyes Skhiri anchoring midfield, but scoring enough goals remains the central question.
Tunisia's coaching transition after AFCON adds cohesion risk.

historical_structural: Sweden have stronger World Cup pedigree and more club-level attacking firepower.
Tunisia have never advanced from the group stage but often compete through compact defensive organization.
With Netherlands and Japan in the same group, Sweden likely need three points here; Tunisia can treat one point as valuable.
